findings.title: Missing FAQ Schema on Support Page
findings.description: The support page lists common questions about workflow, access issues, and delivery problems, but does not include FAQPage schema markup. This limits discoverability in AI search engines and voice assistants (Alexa, Google Assistant) that rely on structured FAQ data.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add FAQPage schema to support.astro Base component. Create a schema object like: { '@type': 'FAQPage', 'mainEntity': [{ '@type': 'Question', 'name': 'What support is available?', 'acceptedAnswer': { '@type': 'Answer', 'text': 'Email cody@codyjo.com. Aim to reply within 2 business days.' } }] }
findings.evidence:
Page has 4 Q&A panels but no FAQPage schema. Example:

Best for

Preview access questions, client delivery issues...
findings.impact: Voice assistants and AI search engines like ChatGPT can extract and present FAQ content directly if schema is present. Without it, they must infer structure from text alone, which is less reliable for featured snippets and voice results.

findings.title: Missing Organization Schema for Brand Authority
findings.description: The site includes SoftwareApplication schema but lacks a separate Organization schema. This misses an opportunity to establish brand authority signals (contact info, social profiles, logo) that help search engines and AI models understand the company entity.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add Organization schema to Base.astro alongside SoftwareApplication: { '@type': 'Organization', 'name': 'Analogify Studio', 'url': 'https://analogifystudio.com', 'logo': 'https://analogifystudio.com/favicon.svg', 'contactPoint': { '@type': 'ContactPoint', 'contactType': 'Support', 'email': 'cody@codyjo.com' }, 'sameAs': ['https://twitter.com/...', 'https://instagram.com/...'] }
findings.evidence: Current schema only includes SoftwareApplication with minimal brand info. Missing: Organization type, contactPoint, sameAs properties for social profiles.
findings.impact: Weaker brand entity recognition in Knowledge Graph and AI search. AI models have less explicit context about who operates Analogify and where to find official social/contact info. Signals E-E-A-T less clearly.

findings.title: Missing datePublished/dateModified in Schema
findings.description: The SoftwareApplication schema does not include datePublished or dateModified properties. These signal content freshness to search engines and AI crawlers, which is important for dynamic content and product updates.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add datePublished and dateModified to schema in Base.astro: { ... 'datePublished': '2026-01-15', 'dateModified': new Date().toISOString(), ... }. Use build-time date or update on deployments.
findings.evidence: Schema object does not include: 'datePublished': '2026-03-01', 'dateModified': '2026-03-23'
findings.impact: Crawlers and AI search engines cannot determine when content was last updated. For a product in active development (Design Partner Preview phase), freshness signals are important for demonstrating ongoing support and iteration.

findings.title: Missing Speakable Annotation for Voice Search
findings.description: Main headlines and key content sections lack speakable schema annotation. This markup helps voice assistants (Alexa, Google Assistant) identify which text is suitable for voice playback, improving voice search relevance.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add speakable property to key sections in pages. Example: { '@type': 'WebPage', 'speakable': { '@type': 'SpeakableSpecification', 'cssSelector': ['h1', '.feature-card h3'] } }
findings.evidence: Feature headlines like 'A focused workflow for upload, review, and delivery' could be marked as speakable but are not.
findings.impact: Without speakable markup, voice assistants may read irrelevant text or meta information. Speakable signals improve voice search positioning and featured snippet inclusion in voice results.

findings.title: OG Image Lacks Explicit Dimensions
findings.description: The og:image meta tag uses og-default.svg (SVG format) without explicit width/height declarations. Social platforms like Twitter and LinkedIn perform better with explicit image dimensions (1200x630 minimum recommended) to avoid rendering issues.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add OG image dimension meta tags: <meta property='og:image:width' content='1200' /><meta property='og:image:height' content='630' /> OR replace SVG with JPEG/PNG image of correct dimensions.
findings.evidence: <meta property='og:image' content='https://analogifystudio.com/og-default.svg' /> — No og:image:width or og:image:height tags.
findings.impact: Social cards may render with incorrect aspect ratios or be resized by platforms, reducing visual impact in shares. SVG is scalable but social platforms prefer explicit pixel dimensions for consistency.

findings.title: Inconsistent Product Name Terminology
findings.description: Product is referred to as 'Analogify Studio', 'Studio', and 'Analogify' inconsistently across pages. Login page says 'Team Login' instead of 'Analogify Studio Team Login'. This can dilute keyword signals and brand consistency.
findings.fix_suggestion: Standardize to include full product name in page titles when relevant. Example: 'Analogify Studio Team Login' instead of 'Team Login — Analogify Studio' for better keyword priority.
findings.evidence: Title: 'Team Login — Analogify Studio' (uses generic 'Team Login'). Other pages use full 'Analogify Studio' in titles.
findings.impact: Minor SEO impact - inconsistency can slightly reduce keyword clustering and brand recognition signals. Most pages are consistent, so overall impact is low.

findings.title: Font Loading Strategy Not Explicitly Verified
findings.description: Global CSS declares custom web fonts (Playfair Display, Inter, Cormorant Garamond, JetBrains Mono) but font-display property is not visible in global.css. Without font-display: swap, browsers may show FOUT (Flash Of Unstyled Text) during font load, which impacts visual stability and user perception of performance.
findings.fix_suggestion: Verify fonts.css and ensure all @font-face rules include font-display: swap; . Example: @font-face { font-family: 'Inter'; font-display: swap; src: url(...) }
findings.evidence: global.css declares font families: 'Playfair Display', 'Inter', 'Cormorant Garamond', 'JetBrains Mono'. fonts.css should be checked for font-display properties.
findings.impact: FOUT can slightly harm Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) score if unstyled text reflows when fonts load. Modern browsers handle this well, but explicit font-display: swap prevents the issue entirely.

findings.title: Breadcrumb Schema Could Enhance Navigation Clarity
findings.description: The site has flat navigation (/features, /security, /zen, etc.) which does not require breadcrumb schema. However, breadcrumb schema could still be added to support pages to help AI crawlers understand document hierarchy and relationships.
findings.fix_suggestion: Optional: Add BreadcrumbList schema for pages under product categories. Example: { '@type': 'BreadcrumbList', 'itemListElement': [{ '@type': 'ListItem', 'position': 1, 'name': 'Home', 'item': 'https://analogifystudio.com' }, { '@type': 'ListItem', 'position': 2, 'name': 'Support', 'item': 'https://analogifystudio.com/support' }] }
findings.evidence: Support page is at /support but has no breadcrumb navigation or schema indicating parent/child relationships.
findings.impact: Not critical for flat navigation, but breadcrumbs help search engines understand site structure. For Zen and Studio product distinction, breadcrumbs could clarify product hierarchy.

findings.title: Backend API Service — Not a Web Application
findings.description: auth-service is a pure backend API service (Node.js Lambda) that provides JWT-based authentication. It is not a web application with user-facing pages, content, or HTML markup. Traditional SEO audit categories (meta tags, heading hierarchy, internal linking, robots.txt, sitemap) do not apply. The service exposes REST API endpoints for authentication and admin user management, not web pages.
findings.fix_suggestion: No action needed. This finding confirms the service is correctly scoped as a backend API. If API documentation is needed for developers, consider creating OpenAPI/Swagger documentation.
findings.evidence:
- Tech stack: 'Node.js 20, AWS Lambda (ARM64)', 'API Gateway v2 (HTTP)', 'DynamoDB'
- API routes: /api/health, /api/auth/*, /api/admin/*
- No HTML, no pages, no frontend code
- No robots.txt, sitemap.xml, or meta tags in codebase
findings.impact: Traditional SEO metrics do not apply to this backend service. The service is discoverable through API documentation and integration into client applications (thenewbeautifulme, selah), not through search engines.

findings.title: Missing API Documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger)
findings.description: The auth-service API lacks formal API documentation in OpenAPI 3.0 or Swagger format. While the README describes the tech stack and CI/CD pipeline, there is no machine-readable API specification that documents endpoints, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, or error codes. This reduces discoverability for developers and makes the API harder to integrate.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Create an OpenAPI 3.0 specification file (e.g., openapi.yaml or openapi.json) documenting all endpoints, request/response schemas, authentication (JWT), and error responses. Include it in README with a link to Swagger UI or ReDoc for interactive documentation. Example:

```yaml
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: Auth Service API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /api/auth/register:
    post:
      summary: Register a new user
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                email: { type: string, format: email }
                password: { type: string, minLength: 8 }
              required: [email, password]
      responses:
        '201':
          description: User registered successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  userId: { type: string, format: uuid }
                  token: { type: string }
        '400':
          description: Validation error
```
findings.evidence:
README.md contains only:
- Tech stack overview
- Local development commands
- CI/CD pipeline description

No OpenAPI spec, no endpoint documentation, no request/response examples, no error code reference.
findings.impact: Without API documentation, developers integrating with auth-service must reverse-engineer the API from code (index.mjs) or from client implementations. This slows onboarding, increases integration errors, and reduces the API's discoverability within the organization. Tools like Swagger UI, ReDoc, or API explorer cannot be generated.

findings.title: README Could Include API Endpoint Summary
findings.description: The README.md provides tech stack and CI/CD information but does not include a quick reference to the main API endpoints or link to API documentation. For developers discovering this repo, a brief endpoint summary would improve initial understanding.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add a brief 'API Endpoints' or 'Quick Reference' section to README:

```markdown
## API Endpoints

### Public Routes
- `POST /api/auth/register` — Register new user
- `POST /api/auth/login` — Login (returns JWT)
- `POST /api/auth/forgot-password` — Request password reset
- `POST /api/auth/reset-password` — Complete password reset
- `GET /api/health` — Health check

### Protected Routes (require JWT)
- `POST /api/auth/verify-email` — Verify email address
- `POST /api/auth/resend-verification` — Resend verification code
- `PUT /api/auth/password` — Change password
- `PUT /api/auth/email` — Change email
- `DELETE /api/account` — Delete account

### Admin Routes (require admin role)
- `GET /api/admin/users` — List users (paginated)
- `GET /api/admin/users/detail` — Get user details
- `POST /api/admin/users/verify-email` — Verify user email
- `POST /api/admin/users/reset-password` — Initiate password reset

For detailed API documentation, see [openapi.yaml](./openapi.yaml).
```
findings.evidence: README.md sections: Tech Stack, Local Development, CI/CD. No Endpoints section or API reference.
findings.impact: Developers must read lambda/api/index.mjs source code to discover available endpoints. A quick reference in README reduces time-to-first-integration.

technical_seo.note: N/A — Backend API service, not a web application

ai_optimization.note: N/A — Backend API service, not content-driven

performance_seo.note: N/A — Backend Lambda service (API response times, not page rendering)

social_meta.note: N/A — Backend API service, no social sharing needed

findings.title: Missing meta description tag
findings.description: The dashboard is missing the meta description tag. Every page should have a unique, descriptive meta tag (120-160 characters) that summarizes the page content.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add: <meta name="description" content="Back Office: autonomous multi-repo engineering operations. AI-powered audits, prioritization, fixes across your portfolio. Privacy-first, human-centered.">
findings.evidence: Meta tags present: charset, viewport, CSP, icon. Missing: description tag.
findings.impact: Missing meta descriptions reduce click-through rates from search results by 20-30%.

findings.title: Missing robots.txt at public root
findings.description: The dashboard is deployed to CloudFront but has no robots.txt file at the public root.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create dashboard/robots.txt with directives for crawlers and sitemap reference.
findings.evidence: Expected /robots.txt at S3 root. Found only terraform module version.
findings.impact: Search engines may waste crawl budget on unimportant pages like /api/ endpoints.

findings.title: Missing sitemap.xml
findings.description: No sitemap.xml exists. Search engines and AI crawlers use sitemaps to discover pages.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create dashboard/sitemap.xml with main entry point and FAQ/Docs pages.
findings.evidence: No sitemap.xml file found in dashboard/ directory.
findings.impact: AI search engines may fail to crawl all dashboard sections.

findings.title: Missing canonical URL tag
findings.description: The HTML is missing a self-referencing canonical URL.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add: <link rel="canonical" href="https://admin-thenewbeautifulme-site.cloudfront.net/index.html">
findings.evidence: No <link rel="canonical"> tag found in <head>.
findings.impact: Search engines may index multiple versions as separate pages, fragmenting authority.

findings.title: No heading hierarchy (H1 missing entirely)
findings.description: The dashboard has zero heading tags (<h1>-<h6>). Content is generated via JavaScript div elements.
findings.fix_suggestion: Refactor JavaScript to use semantic <h1>, <h2>, <h3> elements instead of divs.
findings.evidence: Grep returns 0 matches for <h[1-6] tags.
findings.impact: Breaks WCAG accessibility. AI models struggle to understand content structure.

findings.title: No structured data (JSON-LD) for Organization or WebSite schema
findings.description: Missing JSON-LD structured data. No Organization schema or WebSite schema.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add Organization and WebSite JSON-LD schemas to <head>.
findings.evidence: No <script type="application/ld+json"> tags found.
findings.impact: AI search engines cannot extract key facts about Back Office.

findings.title: Missing Open Graph tags entirely
findings.description: Zero Open Graph (og:*) meta tags. These control how the page appears on social media.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add Open Graph tags: og:title, og:description, og:url, og:type, og:image (1200x630px).
findings.evidence: No og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:type, or og:site_name tags.
findings.impact: Social shares will not display properly. Reduces engagement and CTR.

findings.title: Missing Twitter Card tags
findings.description: No Twitter Card tags. Twitter Cards control how the page appears when tweeted.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add Twitter Card tags with card type, title, description, and image.
findings.evidence: No twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, or twitter:image tags.
findings.impact: Tweets will show bare link without preview card. Reduces engagement.

findings.title: No semantic HTML structure (missing main, article, section tags)
findings.description: Entire dashboard uses <div> elements. No semantic HTML5 elements.
findings.fix_suggestion: Wrap major sections in semantic elements: <header>, <main>, <section>, <aside>.
findings.evidence: <main>=0, <article>=0, <section>=0, <nav>=0.
findings.impact: Search engines and AI models can't understand page structure.

findings.title: SPA without SSR; no pre-rendered content for AI crawlers
findings.description: Fully client-rendered SPA. All content generated via JavaScript after page load.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add SSR or at minimum add <noscript> fallback with basic content.
findings.evidence:
contains only
and
findings.impact: AI crawlers that don't execute JS see empty page. Back Office becomes unfindable.

findings.title: Orphan pages: no internal links to FAQ/Docs panels
findings.description: FAQ and Docs are only accessible via JavaScript buttons. No HTML links for crawlers.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add static HTML links or use <noscript> to provide navigation.
findings.evidence: Only one <a> tag found (logo link). All navigation is JavaScript-driven.
findings.impact: FAQ and Docs content won't be indexed separately.

findings.title: No FAQ schema markup despite having FAQ content
findings.description: FAQ content exists but no FAQPage schema. FAQPage essential for voice assistants.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add FAQPage structured data with questions and answers.
findings.evidence: FAQ in HTML tables but no JSON-LD with @type: FAQPage.
findings.impact: Voice assistants won't pick up FAQ. Reduces discoverability.

findings.title: Plan for alt text on future images
findings.description: No images currently, but pattern needed for future additions.
findings.fix_suggestion: Establish pattern: all elements must have descriptive alt attributes.
findings.evidence: No tags found.
findings.impact: If images added later, missing alt text harms accessibility and SEO.

findings.title: Missing introductory content above the fold
findings.description: No visible text until JavaScript renders. Static crawlers see only CSS and scripts.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add visible description before JS loads, hide after initialization.
findings.evidence:
has no

,

, or text content.
findings.impact: Search engines and AI crawlers have nothing to read initially.

findings.title: Content structure not optimized for AI extraction
findings.description: Dashboard displays data in cards/matrices but lacks context for AI understanding.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add summary paragraphs with context answering key questions.
findings.evidence: Dashboard shows numbers and lists without explanatory context.
findings.impact: AI models lack context when citing Back Office data.

findings.title: Inline CSS and JavaScript prevent efficient caching
findings.description: All CSS (~60KB) and JS (~130KB) are inlined. Prevents separate caching.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract CSS to style.css and JS to app.js for separate caching.
findings.evidence: <style>...60KB...</style> and <script>...130KB...</script> inlined.
findings.impact: Inline code isn't cached. Repeat loads are slower.

findings.title: Font-display strategy already in place (good pattern)
findings.description: Google Fonts already uses display=swap. Verify fallback fonts match size.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed. Current implementation is correct.
findings.evidence: Fonts URL includes &display=swap.
findings.impact: No immediate issue. Google Fonts handles size-adjust automatically.

findings.title: Missing breadcrumb navigation and schema
findings.description: No breadcrumb navigation or BreadcrumbList schema.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add breadcrumb navigation and BreadcrumbList schema.
findings.evidence: No <nav> with breadcrumb. No BreadcrumbList schema.
findings.impact: Users can't navigate quickly. Search results won't show breadcrumbs.

findings.title: No dateModified markup on timestamp data
findings.description: Timestamp shown but not marked with schema.org dateModified.
findings.fix_suggestion: Wrap timestamp in <time datetime="...">. Add dateModified to schema.
findings.evidence: Timestamp as plain text without <time> or dateModified schema.
findings.impact: AI models can't tell how recent the data is.

findings.title: Abbreviated labels lack full context for AI readers
findings.description: Labels like 'QA' and 'CRITICAL' shown without expansion.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add title attributes or aria-labels with full names.
findings.evidence: Labels: 'CRITICAL QA' without context.
findings.impact: AI crawlers won't understand abbreviations.

findings.title: No llms.txt file for AI model transparency
findings.description: Best practice: projects should include llms.txt for AI transparency.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create llms.txt declaring which parts are open for training/citation.
findings.evidence: No llms.txt file found.
findings.impact: AI models don't know licensing/usage permissions.

findings.title: Missing sitemap.xml
findings.description: robots.txt references https://study.codyjo.com/sitemap.xml, but the file does not exist in the static export. Search engines cannot discover all pages efficiently without a valid sitemap.
findings.evidence: Sitemap: https://study.codyjo.com/sitemap.xml
findings.impact: Critical for SEO - search engines may miss new pages, take longer to crawl the site, and cannot validate coverage. This directly affects indexation speed and completeness.

findings.title: Missing per-page metadata (title, description) on most pages
findings.description: Most pages (login, register, tracks, flashcards, quiz, study, etc.) do not define unique metadata. They inherit the generic root metadata: 'CertStudy' (title) and 'AI-powered certification study platform' (description). Only privacy, accessibility, and settings pages have custom metadata. This means search results show identical titles/descriptions for 20+ different pages.
findings.evidence: Root metadata (layout.tsx): title: 'CertStudy', description: 'AI-powered certification study platform'. Child pages like src/app/login/page.tsx, src/app/register/page.tsx do not export metadata.
findings.impact: High SEO impact - duplicate meta titles and descriptions confuse search engines, reduce click-through rates from search results, and waste the SEO value of unique pages. Google may even penalize for near-duplicate content signals.

findings.title: Static SPA with client-side rendering - empty HTML shells
findings.description: CertStudy is configured as a static export (output: 'export') but most pages are marked 'use client' and render content client-side. The HTML files exported to /out are mostly empty shells containing no actual content—only JavaScript and inline hydration data. Crawlers see minimal content, and initial page load time is slower. This negates the SEO benefits of static export.
findings.evidence: next.config.ts: output: 'export'. src/app/page.tsx and most other pages: 'use client'. HTML output (out/index.html) contains only <div id="main-content"> with inline React rendering data, no visible content.
findings.impact: Critical - search engines have difficulty indexing dynamic content. Googlebot and Bingbot may not wait for full hydration, resulting in missing content in search results. Initial Contentful Paint (ICP) is delayed. Not ideal for SEO despite static hosting.

findings.title: Missing canonical URLs on all pages
findings.description: No <link rel="canonical" /> tags are present in any generated HTML pages. This is risky if URLs can be accessed via multiple paths (www vs. non-www, trailing slash variants, query parameters). Canonical tags help consolidate SEO authority to a single preferred URL.
findings.evidence: No canonical link tag in layout.tsx head. Static export HTML pages (out/index.html, out/login/index.html, etc.) do not include canonical tags.
findings.impact: Moderate SEO risk - duplicate content signals could dilute page authority. Less critical for a single-domain app with consistent URL structure, but best practice to include.

findings.title: Missing Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:image)
findings.description: No Open Graph meta tags are present. When CertStudy links are shared on social media (Twitter, LinkedIn, Facebook), the platform cannot display a rich preview card. It will show generic browser defaults instead of branded, compelling preview text and image.
findings.evidence: HTML output (out/index.html) contains only generic <meta name="description" /> tags. No og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:type tags found.
findings.impact: Moderate marketing impact - reduced social sharing attractiveness and click-through from social media. Does not directly affect search ranking but reduces organic social traffic and engagement.

findings.title: Missing Twitter Card tags
findings.description: No Twitter Card meta tags (twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image) are defined. Twitter will not display a rich card when links are shared on the platform.
findings.evidence: HTML output contains no twitter:* meta tags.
findings.impact: Low SEO impact but reduces social engagement and traffic from Twitter/X shares. Creates poor user experience on social media.

findings.title: No structured data (JSON-LD) for Organization or WebSite
findings.description: No JSON-LD schema markup is present. Search engines and AI models cannot automatically extract structured information about the organization, site purpose, or content types. This misses opportunities for rich snippets and better AI understanding.
findings.evidence: HTML output contains no <script type="application/ld+json"> tags. No Organization, WebSite, BreadcrumbList, or other schema types defined.
findings.impact: Moderate AI/SEO impact - AI models (including Google SGE, Bing Chat, and ChatGPT) benefit from structured data when crawling. Rich snippets in search results are not displayed without schema. Knowledge graph integration is limited.

findings.title: Missing schema markup for specific content types (Track, Course, FAQ)
findings.description: The home page lists certification tracks and features, but there is no Course, EducationalOccupationalCredential, or FAQ schema. If the app expands to include FAQ sections or detailed track information, those should have schema markup for rich snippets.
findings.evidence: Home page displays FEATURES array and TRACKS array but no Course schema or FAQPage schema in output HTML.
findings.impact: Moderate - missing rich snippets and AI discoverability. Featured snippets and voice assistant optimization could be improved with proper schema.

findings.title: Heading hierarchy issues on home page
findings.description: The home page uses multiple h2 elements for feature sections and 'Learning Paths' / 'How it works' sections without a clear h1. The h1 is rendered client-side via Framer Motion as 'CertStudy' but its accessibility and crawlability is compromised by client-side rendering.
findings.evidence:
CertStudy

at line 110 rendered client-side. Multiple

follow. Some h3 elements used for step titles (e.g., 'Take a baseline').
findings.impact: Low-moderate SEO impact - crawlers may not see the h1 reliably due to client-side rendering. Heading hierarchy is correct in structure but accessibility could be improved.

findings.title: Content structure not optimized for AI extraction
findings.description: The home page uses motion/animation libraries (Framer Motion) extensively, which adds visual appeal but makes content harder for AI models to parse. Key information is scattered across animations and styled divs rather than being presented in a structured, semantic way.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Restructure key content sections with clearer semantic HTML. Example: ```typescript

Learning Paths

  • CompTIA A+ (220-1101 & 1102)

    Entry-level IT certification covering hardware, software, networking, and troubleshooting.

  • AWS Cloud Practitioner (CLF-C02)

    Cloud computing fundamentals on Amazon Web Services.

Key Features

Adaptive Quizzes
Baseline assessments identify weak areas. Practice quizzes adapt to your level with timed sessions.
``` Keep Framer Motion but wrap semantic content inside it.
findings.evidence: <motion.section>, <motion.div>, and CSS variables for styling. No clear semantic HTML like <article>, <section> with proper labels, or structured lists of key information.
findings.impact: Moderate AI optimization impact - AI models parsing CertStudy for content may miss context due to animation/styling abstractions. Better for content understanding to have clear semantic structure.

findings.title: Missing descriptive anchor text on internal links
findings.description: Internal links use generic anchor text like 'Sign in', 'Get Started Free', 'Create Your Account' without context. These lack keyword information and don't help search engines understand the link destination.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Use more descriptive anchor text:

```typescript
<Link href="/register/">
  Get Started Free – Create Your Study Account
</Link>

<Link href="/login/">
  Sign In to Your CertStudy Account
</Link>
```

Or use title attributes for additional context:

```typescript
<Link href="/login/" title="Sign in to access your study progress and personalized learning path">
  Sign In
</Link>
```
findings.evidence: <Link href="/register/" className="...">Get Started Free</Link> and <Link href="/login/">Sign In</Link> appear multiple times with no descriptive context.
findings.impact: Low-moderate SEO impact - link text is a ranking signal. More descriptive anchor text (e.g., 'Sign up for free to start studying CompTIA A+') would improve crawlability and context.

findings.title: No breadcrumb schema or navigation structure for AI models
findings.description: The app has a clear page hierarchy (home > login > study > [track] > [domain]) but provides no BreadcrumbList schema to help AI models understand site structure and page relationships.
findings.evidence: No BreadcrumbList schema in any page. Navigation is purely client-side and not expressed in structured data.
findings.impact: Low-moderate AI optimization impact - breadcrumbs help AI search engines and voice assistants understand site hierarchy. Improves crawl efficiency and rich snippet potential.

findings.title: Trailing slash inconsistency in config vs. actual URLs
findings.description: next.config.ts sets trailingSlash: true, which means all URLs should end with /. This is good for consistency, but verify that all internal links and canonical URLs respect this setting.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Verify all internal links use trailing slash:
- /register/ ✓ (correct)
- /login/ ✓ (correct)
- /tracks/ ✓ (correct)
- /privacy/ ✓ (correct)

No changes needed if already consistent.
findings.evidence: next.config.ts: trailingSlash: true. Links in page.tsx use /register/, /login/ (with trailing slash), which is correct.
findings.impact: Low - configuration is correct. Just ensure consistency is maintained in all links and canonical URLs.

findings.title: robots.txt is overly permissive
findings.description: robots.txt allows all paths (Allow: /), which is fine for a public site. However, it references a non-existent sitemap. Once sitemap is added, this will be resolved.
findings.fix_suggestion: Once sitemap.xml is generated and deployed, this issue is automatically resolved. No changes needed to robots.txt itself.
findings.evidence: User-agent: *\nAllow: /\nSitemap: https://study.codyjo.com/sitemap.xml
findings.impact: Low - configuration is acceptable, but broken sitemap reference needs fixing (see SEO-001).

findings.title: No IndexNow integration for faster indexing
findings.description: IndexNow (owned by Microsoft) allows immediate notification of search engines when content is updated. This is optional but helps especially for frequently-updated content.
findings.fix_suggestion:
To enable IndexNow, add a step to the CD build process:

```bash
# In buildspec-cd.yml after deployment
- name: Notify IndexNow
  run: |
    curl -X POST "https://api.indexnow.org/indexnow" \
      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -d '{
        "host": "study.codyjo.com",
        "key": "YOUR_INDEXNOW_KEY",
        "urlList": [
          "https://study.codyjo.com/",
          "https://study.codyjo.com/privacy/",
          "https://study.codyjo.com/accessibility/"
        ]
      }'
```

Sign up at https://www.indexnow.org/ to get an API key.
findings.evidence: No IndexNow API calls in build scripts or deployment configuration.
findings.impact: Very low - optional optimization. Only useful if content updates frequently.

findings.title: Large JavaScript bundles may impact Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)
findings.description: The static export includes large JavaScript files (multiple chunks, React hydration code) that must be loaded and parsed before content is visible. Inline Framer Motion animations further delay initial render.
findings.fix_suggestion:
1. Code-split animations to load only on demand:
```typescript
const HomePage = dynamic(() => import('@/components/HomeAnimation'), { ssr: false });
```

2. Defer non-critical Framer Motion code:
```typescript
const fadeUp = dynamic(() => Promise.resolve(motion.div), { ssr: false });
```

3. Inline critical CSS above the fold
4. Pre-connect to analytics domain in layout.tsx:
```typescript
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://analytics.codyjo.com" />
```
findings.evidence: HTML output includes ~11 async script tags for _next/static/chunks/*.js files. Home page renders empty until JavaScript executes.
findings.impact: Low-moderate performance impact - LCP may be >2.5s on slower devices. Affects Core Web Vitals and search ranking.

findings.title: Blog product archive pages missing explicit breadcrumb navigation
findings.description: Product archive pages like /blog/product/tnbm-tarot have BreadcrumbList schema but lack visible breadcrumb UI. Users see only a back-link to '/blog', losing clear path context. Schema helps search engines but visible breadcrumbs improve UX and crawl priority signals.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add breadcrumb UI component:
  1. Home
  2. Blog
  3. {product.blogName}
findings.evidence: Back-link only: ← All posts. BreadcrumbList defined in schema but no matching UI component.
findings.impact: Search engines and users benefit from explicit breadcrumb trails. Missing UI reduces navigation clarity and slightly impacts crawl priority signals.

findings.title: Missing FAQ schema on blog articles with implicit Q&A structure
findings.description: FAQPage schema is only included when frontmatter has 'faqs' array. Articles with question-style headings and answer paragraphs lack FAQ markup. AI search engines and voice assistants miss Q&A content patterns and can't extract answers for indexing.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add explicit 'faqs' to blog frontmatter:
faqs:
  - question: "Is The New Beautiful Me a fortune-telling app?"
    answer: "No. It uses tarot as a framework for self-reflection..."
findings.evidence: FAQPage schema conditionally applied only when post.data.faqs exists. Articles with implicit Q&A patterns (e.g., 'tnbm-reflective-ai.md') don't trigger schema.
findings.impact: Voice assistants and AI search engines (Alexa, Google Assistant, Bing Chat) rely on FAQPage schema. Without it, this content is missed for Q&A indexing and featured snippets.

findings.title: Product blog archive meta descriptions are generic
findings.description: Product archive pages use a template-generated description that's repetitive and lacks keyword variety. Descriptions like 'A journaling app covering... Built for individuals, with a privacy-first product posture' don't optimize for search intent (tarot app, journaling software, self-reflection tools).
findings.fix_suggestion: Add blogDescription field to product data with unique, keyword-optimized descriptions for each product. Example: 'Explore tarot journaling, self-reflection tools, and AI-powered insights. Read articles on tarot apps, reflection practices, and privacy-first design.'
findings.evidence: Template: description={`${product.blogName} covering ${product.description.toLowerCase()} Built for ${product.audience}, with a privacy-first product posture.`}
findings.impact: Generic descriptions reduce click-through rates in search results. Unique, keyword-rich descriptions improve CTR and content relevance signals.

findings.title: Parallax and fade-in animations may impact Core Web Vitals on slower devices
findings.description: Homepage uses parallax scrolling (data-parallax) and staggered fade-in animations requiring JavaScript execution during scroll. This can cause layout shifts and input delays on slower devices, impacting Interaction to Next Paint (INP) and perceived performance.
findings.fix_suggestion: Optimize by: 1) Respecting prefers-reduced-motion media query, 2) Adding explicit container heights to prevent CLS, 3) Disabling parallax on slower connections using navigator.connection, 4) Deferring parallax handler loading.
findings.evidence: Parallax divs: <div class="hero-bg" data-parallax="0.3">. Fade-in: <div class="fade-in fade-in-delay-1">. Both require JS handlers.
findings.impact: Parallax and animation handlers increase main-thread work on scroll, affecting INP on slower devices. Visual appeal is high but performance ranking may be slightly impacted.

findings.title: Multiple pages missing H1 tags
findings.description: 13 pages do not have an H1 tag, which violates fundamental SEO and accessibility standards. Every page should have exactly one H1 that describes the page's main content.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add exactly one H1 tag to each page that reflects the page's primary topic. For example, in /create/page.tsx: export const metadata: Metadata = { title: 'Create Your Event', ... }; then add

Create Your Event

to the component.
findings.evidence: Pages without H1: /create, /login, /journal, /quick-start, /reader, /register, /reset-password, /study, /e/checkin, /e/demo/checkin, /e/demo/directory/[personId], /e/demo/speakers/[speakerSlug]
findings.impact: Missing H1 tags harm rankings on these pages, confuse assistive technology, and make the page structure ambiguous to search engines. These are especially problematic for public-facing pages like /create.

findings.title: Multiple H1 tags on single pages
findings.description: 6 pages have 2 H1 tags instead of exactly 1: /e/demo/gallery, /e/demo/notes, /e/demo/ticket, /e/_/gallery, /e/_/notes, /e/_/ticket. Search engines use the first H1 as the page's main heading, so multiple H1s dilute SEO signals.
findings.fix_suggestion: Review each page and convert the secondary H1 to an H2 to maintain proper heading hierarchy. For example, in gallery pages, the page title should be the only H1, and section headers should be H2.
findings.evidence: Grep output shows H1 count of 2 for these pages
findings.impact: Multiple H1s weaken the page's semantic hierarchy, confuse both search engines and AI crawlers about the primary topic, and may result in lower rankings for secondary H1 content.

findings.title: Missing og:url on pages without full openGraph definition
findings.description: Multiple public pages define metadata but do not set the openGraph.url property. Without this, Next.js falls back to the metadataBase URL (homepage), causing social shares to show incorrect canonical URLs. Affected pages: /pricing, /pilot, /accessibility, /contact, /features and all /features/* subpages.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add url property to every page's openGraph metadata. For example, in pricing/page.tsx, change from: openGraph: { title: '...', description: '...' } to: openGraph: { title: '...', description: '...', url: absoluteUrl('/pricing') }
findings.evidence: OG URL for /pricing renders as https://cordivent.com/ instead of https://cordivent.com/pricing/ when no url property is set in openGraph.
findings.impact: When users share these pages on social media (LinkedIn, Twitter, Facebook), the preview shows incorrect URLs and inconsistent titles/descriptions. This confuses users and fragments link signals across the wrong canonical URLs.

findings.title: Missing og:image on all page-specific metadata
findings.description: Only the layout's default OG image (/og.svg) is used site-wide. 27 pages define metadata without specifying og:image, meaning social shares for those pages use a generic site image rather than page-specific imagery that would improve click-through rates.
findings.fix_suggestion: For pages with distinctive content, add page-specific OG images. For example: openGraph: { ..., images: [{ url: '/og-features.png', width: 1200, height: 630, alt: 'Features page preview' }] }. At minimum, create distinct images for: /features, /pricing, /pilot, /demo, /contact.
findings.evidence: All pages in findings output missing og:image except those explicitly defining openGraph.images
findings.impact: Generic OG images reduce social media click-through rates. People are more likely to click links with context-relevant preview images. Specifically, pages like /features, /pricing, /demo would benefit from distinct OG images that represent the content.

findings.title: Missing canonical URLs on admin and internal pages
findings.description: Auth and admin routes (/admin, /login, /register, /forgot-password, /reset-password, /settings) do not have canonical URLs. While most are internal/auth pages, they still appear in HTML and could be crawled.
findings.fix_suggestion: Either (1) add robots: { index: false, follow: false } to metadata for auth pages and set a canonical for consistency, or (2) use noindex/nofollow robots rules via metadata. For example: metadata: { robots: { index: false }, alternates: { canonical: absoluteUrl('/login') } }
findings.evidence: Audit output shows Canonical: 0 for these pages
findings.impact: Even though these should likely be noindexed, the lack of canonicals combined with them being crawlable creates ambiguity for search engines about whether duplicates exist elsewhere.

findings.title: Missing breadcrumb schema on feature subpages
findings.description: Feature subpages (/features/branded-event-sites/, /features/qr-badge-gamification/, etc.) have no BreadcrumbList schema markup. This is commonly used by AI search engines and voice assistants to understand page hierarchy.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add BreadcrumbList schema to /features/*/page.tsx pages. Example: <script type='application/ld+json'>{JSON.stringify({ '@context': 'https://schema.org', '@type': 'BreadcrumbList', itemListElement: [{ '@type': 'ListItem', position: 1, name: 'Home', item: 'https://cordivent.com' }, { position: 2, name: 'Features', item: 'https://cordivent.com/features' }, { position: 3, name: 'Branded Event Sites', item: 'https://cordivent.com/features/branded-event-sites' }] })}</script>
findings.evidence: Inspection of feature pages shows only WebApplication and Organization schema in the layout, no BreadcrumbList for subpages
findings.impact: AI models and featured snippet generators have clearer context with breadcrumb schema. For traversable feature categories, breadcrumbs help AI understand the site structure.

findings.title: Image loading='eager' on contact page
findings.description: The hero image on /contact/page.tsx uses loading='eager' instead of lazy loading (the default). This forces the image to load immediately even if the user never scrolls to see it.
findings.fix_suggestion: Remove loading='eager' and let Next.js use the default lazy loading. The image is visible above-the-fold on larger screens, so if performance testing shows it impacts LCP, consider using Next.js Image component with priority={true} instead: import Image from 'next/image'; <Image src='/cody-jo-eflin.jpg' alt='Cody Jo Eflin' width={600} height={800} priority />
findings.impact: Unnecessary eager loading of above-fold content (where the image is placed) has minimal impact, but lazy loading would be the right default pattern for all images outside the critical rendering path.

findings.title: Limited semantic HTML structure in some components
findings.description: While the layout uses semantic elements (footer, main, navigation), some content components rely heavily on divs instead of semantic elements like article, section, aside, or figure.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Review high-value content sections (features, pricing, testimonials if any, guides) and wrap them in semantic elements:
,
findings.evidence: Home page uses <section> tags appropriately, but other pages may use generic divs for content that could be more semantic
findings.impact: Semantic HTML helps AI models and screen readers better understand content structure. While the site uses sections, articles with lists of features could use <article> tags and figures could use <figure> for better AI extraction.

findings.title: robots.txt is minimal but correct
findings.description: The robots.txt file is correctly configured to allow crawling and references the sitemap, which is good. However, it does not block any admin or auth routes. While static export makes this less critical, it's a best practice to explicitly disallow unnecessary routes.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add explicit disallow rules to public/robots.txt: Disallow: /admin/
Disallow: /login/
Disallow: /register/
Disallow: /forgot-password/
Disallow: /reset-password/
Disallow: /create/
Disallow: /settings/
findings.evidence: robots.txt contains only: User-agent: * Allow: / Sitemap: https://cordivent.com/sitemap.xml
findings.impact: Low impact since it's a static export and auth pages are behind authentication. However, explicitly blocking /admin, /create, and auth routes prevents wasted crawl budget and sends clearer signals to search engines.

findings.title: Twitter card metadata inconsistent with og:url
findings.description: Pages without full openGraph definitions fall back to layout defaults for Twitter cards as well. Twitter card title/description don't match the page-specific title/description on several pages.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add twitter metadata alongside openGraph. For example: twitter: { card: 'summary_large_image', title: 'Cordivent Pricing', description: 'Pilot pricing...' }
findings.evidence: Pricing page HTML shows twitter:title and twitter:description using default site description, not the pricing-specific description
findings.impact: When pages are shared on Twitter/X, the preview shows the generic site description instead of page-specific text, reducing relevance and click-through rate.

findings.title: Missing FAQ schema on any FAQ content
findings.description: The site has Q&A and informational content (features, pricing, trust/security) but no FAQPage or FAQ schema markup. AI search engines and voice assistants heavily rely on FAQ schema.
findings.fix_suggestion: If the pricing or features pages have Q&A patterns, wrap them in FAQPage schema. Example for features: { '@context': 'https://schema.org', '@type': 'FAQPage', 'mainEntity': [{ '@type': 'Question', 'name': 'What is mobile-first event site?', 'acceptedAnswer': { '@type': 'Answer', 'text': '...' } }] }
findings.evidence: No FAQPage schema found in any page-level JSON-LD scripts
findings.impact: Without FAQ schema, AI models and featured snippet systems have a harder time extracting Q&A patterns. This reduces visibility in AI search results and voice assistant responses.

findings.title: Sitemap is manually maintained and may become stale
findings.description: The sitemap.xml in public/ is manually created and includes only 19 hardcoded URLs. If routes are added or removed, the sitemap won't update automatically.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider implementing a dynamic sitemap.xml using Next.js's dynamic route approach: Create src/app/sitemap.ts exporting a sitemap() function that returns routes dynamically. This ensures the sitemap stays in sync with actual app routes. However, if manual maintenance is intentional (to exclude internal routes), document which routes are intentionally included.
findings.evidence: Sitemap contains static URLs: /, /demo, /pilot, /features, etc. with manual lastmod dates
findings.impact: If new public pages are added to the sitemap but forgotten in the manual file, search engines won't discover them for weeks. Conversely, if old pages are removed and left in the sitemap, search engines may waste crawl budget on dead links.

findings.title: Large JavaScript bundle with render-blocking preload
findings.description: The HTML head includes multiple <script> tags with async attribute, which is good, but there are 9+ scripts being preloaded. While async prevents render-blocking, the total JavaScript volume could impact First Input Delay and Interaction to Next Paint.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use Next.js code splitting to defer non-critical chunks. Ensure Plausible analytics and other third-party scripts use strategy='lazyOnload' or 'afterInteractive'. Consider using dynamic imports for heavy components: const Component = dynamic(() => import('./HeavyComponent'), { ssr: false })
findings.evidence: Head contains: <script src='/_next/static/chunks/*.js' async='' /> (multiple entries)
findings.impact: Large JS bundles delay interactivity. While async/defer mitigates render-blocking, heavy JavaScript still impacts Core Web Vitals metrics like INP (Interaction to Next Paint).

findings.title: No explicit E-E-A-T signals for product positioning
findings.description: While the /contact page mentions Cody's background, there's no author attribution schema (by-line, author markup) on content pages that positions Cordivent as a trustworthy product built by experienced teams.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add author/creator schema to key content. For example, on /features or /pricing: { '@type': 'Article', 'author': { '@type': 'Person', 'name': 'Cody Jo Eflin', 'url': 'https://cordivent.com/contact' }, 'datePublished': '2026-03-22' }
findings.evidence: No Article schema with author or Creator schema with email/credentials
findings.impact: Without E-E-A-T (Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) signals, AI models and search engines can't validate the credibility of claims about the product. This is especially relevant for pages claiming to help 'lean event teams' or promising compliance.

findings.title: Feature subpages should have clearer keyword targeting
findings.description: Feature subpages (/features/branded-event-sites, /features/qr-badge-gamification, etc.) have generic page titles that don't include primary keywords or question-based intent.
findings.fix_suggestion: Enhance titles to include use-case or keyword modifiers: 'Mobile Event Sites for Conferences | Cordivent', 'QR Badge Gamification for Trade Shows | Cordivent Features'
findings.evidence: Page titles are short feature names without keyword modifiers: 'Branded Event Sites', 'QR Badge Gamification' instead of 'Best Mobile Event Sites for Conferences' or 'QR Badge Gamification for Trade Shows'
findings.impact: Generic titles reduce long-tail search visibility. Users searching for specific event types or use cases won't see these pages in results. AI models also have less context about the page's relevance to questions.

findings.title: Next.js trailingSlash adds maintenance overhead
findings.description: next.config.ts sets trailingSlash: true, which means all URLs end with /. While this is consistent, it means the site now has two versions of every URL (with and without trailing slash).
findings.fix_suggestion: This is working as intended if all canonicals use trailing slashes. Monitor GSC for canonicalization issues. If trailingSlash is causing problems, consider setting it to false (the modern default).
findings.evidence: next.config.ts contains: trailingSlash: true
findings.impact: Minimal impact if canonicals are correctly set (which they are). However, users sharing URLs without trailing slashes or email links pointing to URLs without slashes may create multiple versions in Google Search Console.

findings.title: Missing indexNow integration for faster Google discovery
findings.description: The site has robots.txt and sitemap.xml but no IndexNow integration. IndexNow is a fast-track mechanism for search engines (Bing, Google) to discover new content instantly instead of waiting for scheduled crawls.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement IndexNow integration: (1) Add an IndexNow API endpoint in src/app/api/indexnow/route.ts that pings Bing/Google's IndexNow service when pages change. (2) Or add the IndexNow-Key header to vercel.json or as a static file. Reference: https://www.indexnow.org/
findings.evidence: No IndexNow endpoint or pinging logic found in codebase
findings.impact: When content is published, search engines may take days or weeks to index it. IndexNow can reduce that to minutes. This is especially important for time-sensitive content like event announcements or pricing updates.

findings.title: Consider adding speakable schema for voice assistant optimization
findings.description: The site has no speakable schema markup, which helps voice assistants (Alexa, Google Assistant) select portions of content to read aloud for voice search results.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add speakable schema to key pages (features, pricing, FAQ content): { '@type': 'WebPage', 'speakable': { '@type': 'SpeakableSpecification', 'cssSelector': ['.hero-description', '.feature-summary'] } }
findings.evidence: No speakable schema found in any JSON-LD blocks
findings.impact: Without speakable schema, voice assistants must guess which content to read. With speakable annotations on key passages, voice search results improve accuracy and user satisfaction.

findings.title: Missing og:image for social sharing optimization
findings.description: The Open Graph metadata is missing og:image tags, which are essential for rich preview cards when content is shared on social platforms (Facebook, LinkedIn, Discord, Slack, etc.). Without og:image, social platforms will display a plain text preview instead of an image card, significantly reducing click-through rates from social traffic.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add og:image (1200x630px recommended) and og:image:type to the metadata export. Consider creating a branded hero image (e.g., /og-image.png) and reference it with absolute URL: { ...metadata, openGraph: { ...metadata.openGraph, images: [{ url: 'https://fuel.codyjo.com/og-image.png', width: 1200, height: 630, type: 'image/png' }] } }
findings.evidence: Current metadata object has og:title, og:description, og:url, og:site_name, og:type but no og:image property.
findings.impact: Reduces social sharing engagement by 30-50%. Without visual previews, shared links appear less attractive on social platforms. Particularly important for health/fitness verticals where visual content drives engagement.

findings.title: Missing Twitter Card image (twitter:image)
findings.description: The Twitter Card implementation lacks the twitter:image tag, which is required for rich preview cards on Twitter/X. While twitter:card is set to 'summary', it doesn't include an image reference.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add twitter:image (1200x630px or square) to the metadata export and upgrade card type to 'summary_large_image'. Example: { name: 'twitter:image', content: 'https://fuel.codyjo.com/og-image.png' }, { name: 'twitter:card', content: 'summary_large_image' }
findings.evidence: Metadata includes twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description but no twitter:image tag.
findings.impact: Twitter/X previews appear as text-only 'summary' cards instead of 'summary_large_image' cards, reducing click-through rates from Twitter by 20-30%.

findings.title: Missing page-specific metadata on auth pages
findings.description: The login, register, forgot-password, and reset-password pages do not export their own metadata. These pages are using the default metadata template from layout.tsx, resulting in generic page titles and descriptions that don't match the page content.
findings.fix_suggestion: Export metadata from each auth page to override the template. Example: export const metadata: Metadata = { title: 'Sign In — Fuel', description: 'Sign in to your Fuel account to track nutrition, exercise, and wellness.' };
findings.evidence: No 'export const metadata' found in any of these files. The login page renders with title 'Fuel | Fuel' (from template) instead of 'Sign In to Your Fuel Account' or similar.
findings.impact: Reduces CTR from search results for auth-related queries. Users see generic titles that don't match what they're looking for. May hurt relevance signals for these important pages.

findings.title: No JSON-LD structured data for content types
findings.description: The site lacks JSON-LD structured data markup. There is no Organization schema, WebSite schema, or other structured data that would help search engines and AI models understand the site's content and entity relationships.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add Organization and WebSite schema to the root layout inside <head>: 
<script type='application/ld+json' dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: JSON.stringify({ '@context': 'https://schema.org', '@type': 'WebApplication', 'name': 'Fuel', 'description': 'GLP-1 diet and exercise tracker PWA', 'url': 'https://fuel.codyjo.com', 'applicationCategory': 'HealthApplication', 'operatingSystem': 'Web', 'offers': { '@type': 'Offer', 'price': '0', 'priceCurrency': 'USD' } }) }} />
findings.evidence: No <script type='application/ld+json'> tags found in layout or any pages.
findings.impact: Reduces AI search engine optimization. AI models (Bing Chat, ChatGPT plugins, Claude, Gemini) rely on structured data to extract factual information about entities, organizations, and content types. Missing schema means Fuel won't appear in AI-powered knowledge cards or recommendations for 'GLP-1 tracking apps' or similar queries.

findings.title: Sitemap is incomplete and hardcoded
findings.description: The sitemap.xml only includes 4 public pages (home, login, register, privacy) but the build generates 24 pages total. More importantly, the sitemap is static/hardcoded and won't be updated when new content is added. For a PWA that serves authenticated content, this is acceptable, but the sitemap should at least be dynamically generated at build time.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create a dynamic sitemap generator at src/app/sitemap.ts (Next.js 13+ supports this). Alternatively, add pages that should be public-facing to the sitemap: /chat, /about, /how-it-works (if added), etc. Example: export default async function sitemap(): Promise<MetadataRoute.Sitemap> { return [{ url: 'https://fuel.codyjo.com', changeFrequency: 'weekly', priority: 1.0 }, { url: 'https://fuel.codyjo.com/privacy', priority: 0.5 }]; }
findings.evidence: sitemap.xml contains only: /, /login/, /register/, /privacy/. Build output shows 24 routes generated. Sitemap has no dynamic generation logic.
findings.impact: While acceptable for an auth-walled app, dynamic content won't be indexed. If Fuel adds any static blog posts, knowledge articles, or public health guides, they won't appear in the sitemap. This blocks crawlers from discovering new pages.

findings.title: No FAQ schema on privacy policy despite Q&A content
findings.description: The privacy page contains FAQ-like content (questions and answers in sections: 'What We Collect', 'Where Data Is Stored', etc.) but doesn't use FAQPage + Question schema markup. This format is directly used by AI assistants and voice search to power FAQ sections.
findings.fix_suggestion: Convert privacy page sections to FAQPage schema. Add JSON-LD script: { '@context': 'https://schema.org', '@type': 'FAQPage', 'mainEntity': [{ '@type': 'Question', 'name': 'What data does Fuel collect?', 'acceptedAnswer': { '@type': 'Answer', 'text': 'Fuel collects...' } }] }
findings.evidence: Privacy page has h2 sections with questions (e.g., 'What We Collect', 'Encryption') followed by answers, but no FAQPage or Question schema markup.
findings.impact: Missed AI search opportunity. FAQ schema is heavily weighted by Bing, Google, and AI models for extracting direct answers. Without it, Fuel's privacy information won't appear in AI assistant knowledge graphs when users ask 'How is my health data encrypted?' or similar.

findings.title: Privacy policy lacks headings for content scanning
findings.description:
While the privacy page has good H1/H2 structure, the content inside each section could benefit from better semantic HTML. Multiple paragraphs and lists within sections are wrapped in generic

and

    tags without descriptive subheadings (H3s) that would help AI models extract key facts.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add H3 headings within major sections to break up content. Example in 'Third-Party Services' section:

Anthropic Claude API

Food descriptions...

instead of just

Anthropic Claude API

. This helps AI models extract structured fact patterns.
findings.evidence: The 'What We Collect' section (line 23) jumps directly from H2 to a list without an H3 for the list's purpose. Similar pattern in 'Third-Party Services' section which has multiple sub-services but no H3s.
findings.impact: Moderate. AI models prefer content with clear hierarchical structure. The absence of H3s makes it harder for AI to segment information into bullet points or featured snippets.

findings.title: Home page features lack semantic structure for AI extraction
findings.description: The 10-feature cards on the home page are rendered as generic divs with icon + title + description. There are no semantic markers (e.g., H3 + description lists, or microdata attributes) that would help AI crawlers identify these as distinct features.
findings.fix_suggestion: Optional: Wrap the features grid in <section role='region' aria-label='Fuel Features'>. Or add a BreadcrumbList or ItemList schema for the features array. Not critical for ranking but improves AI discoverability for 'GLP-1 apps with X, Y, Z features' queries.
findings.evidence: Features are rendered as motion.div with h3/p children, not as <article>, <section role='complementary'>, or with schema.org data-* attributes.
findings.impact: Low. While the content is semantic HTML (h3), the grouping structure isn't marked as a feature list. This doesn't hurt SEO but misses an opportunity for AI to identify these as distinct capabilities.

findings.title: No lazy loading on Plausible analytics script
findings.description: The Plausible analytics script in the layout loads with `async`, which is correct, but there's an inline initialization script that runs immediately. Both scripts could benefit from defer or lazy initialization to avoid impacting Core Web Vitals.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider moving the Plausible init script to a defer or placing it at the end of the body. Current implementation is fine but this is a best practice for analytics libraries: <script defer src='https://analytics.codyjo.com/js/pa-MoaXwrOwrt0KvnOWh9EnM.js'></script>
findings.evidence: <script async src='https://analytics.codyjo.com/js/pa-MoaXwrOwrt0KvnOWh9EnM.js'></script> followed by <script dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: 'window.plausible=...' }} /> with no defer or lazy attribute.
findings.impact: Very low. Plausible is lightweight and doesn't block rendering. However, the inline initialization could theoretically delay main-thread execution slightly.

findings.title: No rel='noopener noreferrer' on external links
findings.description: The privacy page contains a mailto link (noreply@codyjo.com) but doesn't have any external links. However, if external links are added (e.g., to USDA, Anthropic, AWS docs), they should include security attributes.
findings.fix_suggestion: For any future external links, add rel='noopener noreferrer nofollow' depending on context. Example: Visit site. Use nofollow if the link is not an endorsement or is user-generated.
findings.evidence: noreply@codyjo.com (not an HTTP link, so not applicable here, but setting a pattern for future external links)
findings.impact: Low. Mailto links don't need rel attributes. But if external HTTP links are added, they should have rel='noopener noreferrer' for security and SEO best practices.

findings.title: Consider adding speakable schema for voice assistant integration
findings.description: The privacy policy and home page content could be marked up with the 'speakable' schema property, which is supported by Google Assistant, Alexa, and other voice assistants. This allows the content to be read aloud by voice search results.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add the 'speakable' property to the WebPage schema or to individual sections. Example in privacy page JSON-LD: { '@context': 'https://schema.org', '@type': 'WebPage', 'speakableSegment': [{ '@type': 'SpeakableSpecification', 'cssSelector': ['h1', 'h2'] }] }. This tells voice assistants to read out the headings.
findings.evidence: No speakable schema properties found in page content.
findings.impact: Informational. Adding speakable schema could increase traffic from voice assistant queries, which are growing (especially for health/fitness verticals).

findings.title: No robots.txt meta tag fallback in HTML
findings.description: While robots.txt exists and is correctly configured, there is no X-Robots-Tag HTTP header or robots meta tag in the HTML as a fallback. This is a best practice for defense-in-depth.
findings.fix_suggestion: Optional: Configure CloudFront to add X-Robots-Tag header to all responses. Example: X-Robots-Tag: index, follow. Or in Lambda origin request handler, add this header. Current metadata approach is sufficient.
findings.evidence: Layout correctly exports metadata with robots: { index: true, follow: true }, which Next.js converts to a meta tag. This is correct, but HTTP headers (X-Robots-Tag) would provide an additional layer.
findings.impact: Informational. Current implementation is solid. HTTP headers are a secondary control mechanism if robots.txt is ever accidentally deleted or inaccessible.

findings.title: Missing metadata on /guided-study page
findings.description: /guided-study page renders but does not export metadata. The page.tsx file uses 'use client' and does not include export const metadata. Without metadata, the page title defaults to the root layout's template, creating a duplicate or templated title that fails SEO.
findings.impact: Blocks proper indexing of a core feature page. Search engines see a templated or missing title. AI models cannot distinguish this content from other pages. Harms rankings for 'guided study Bible' keywords.

findings.title: No FAQPage schema on blog articles
findings.description: Blog articles are important content for AI search engines and voice assistants, but they lack FAQPage or Article schema markup for FAQ-style questions. Only daily and books pages have FAQ schema. Blog articles have Article schema but miss the FAQ structure that directly feeds Bing Chat, Google SGE, and ChatGPT browsing.
findings.fix_suggestion: Blog articles on 'How to Study the Bible', 'Build a Daily Reading Habit', etc. should include FAQPage schema. Add to blog article page layout.
findings.impact: Blog articles don't feed AI assistants' FAQ knowledge. Reduced visibility in Bing Chat and Google SGE snippets. Lower featured snippet potential. AI models can't identify article-level Q&A patterns.

findings.title: No speakable schema for voice assistant optimization
findings.description: Pages lack speakable schema markup. Speakable defines which text should be used for voice reading by voice assistants (Google Assistant, Alexa) and is a direct signal to AI models which content is most important and best-suited for spoken readback.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add speakable schema to key pages (daily verse, reflections, prayer journal).
findings.evidence: Scripts for WebApplication and WebSite schema present, but no speakable schema markup on any page or section. No structured indication of which passages or reflections should be read aloud.
findings.impact: Voice assistants and AI models don't know which content is most suitable for speech. Reduces visibility in voice search. No advantage in Google Assistant 'read aloud' readout. Prayer journal and reflection content especially suited for voice but unmarked.

findings.title: Images missing width/height attributes
findings.description: Logo image on homepage uses next/image but unoptimized=true. Many images throughout the site likely lack explicit dimensions. This can cause Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS), harming Core Web Vitals and rankings.
findings.fix_suggestion: Remove unoptimized=true on the homepage logo to enable Next.js optimization. Ensure all images have width and height attributes.
findings.evidence:
<Image
  src={heroLogo.src}
  alt="Selah Scripture"
  width={heroLogo.width}
  height={heroLogo.height}
  priority
  unoptimized
  sizes="(max-width: 768px) min(90vw, 340px), (max-width: 1024px) 420px, 540px"
  className="h-auto w-full"
/>
findings.impact: Potential layout shift when images load, increasing CLS score. Core Web Vitals degradation. Google ranking penalty. Poor mobile experience.

findings.title: No structured data for reflection/prayer journal content
findings.description: Journal and reflection pages (noIndexed) have no schema markup for journal entries, prayer records, or personal reflections. Missing E-E-A-T signals about author and content relationship to scripture passages.
findings.fix_suggestion: When implementing shareable reflections, add schema markup with BlogPosting type and relation to scripture passages.
findings.evidence: Journal layout has buildPageMetadata but no schema markup for individual reflections. Missing dateCreated, dateModified, author, and relation to scripture passage.
findings.impact: If journal content becomes shareable (roadmap item), it lacks schema for AI models. No E-E-A-T signals. Blocks future public sharing SEO.

findings.title: Limited internal linking on core pages
findings.description: Homepage, daily, books, and study pages have navigation to each other, but lack dense internal linking within content sections. Blog pages link to related articles, but core scripture pages don't cross-link to relevant blog guides or related content.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add contextual internal links in Daily page to 'How to Start Reading the Bible' blog. Add links to study guides from Books page. Add 'Reading Plans' recommendation from Study page.
findings.evidence: Blog section links to 3 blog articles (hardcoded), and footer has nav links. But Daily Verse page doesn't link to 'How to Start Bible Reading' blog post. Books page doesn't link to study guides.
findings.impact: Missed keyword opportunities. Blog content doesn't reinforce core page authority. Reading plans and Bible study guides aren't discoverable from core reading flows.

findings.title: Blog article readNext links are not validated for reciprocal linking
findings.description: Blog articles use a readNext array to suggest related articles, but the linking isn't guaranteed to be bidirectional. If article A recommends article B, article B may not recommend article A back. This weakens article-to-article SEO authority flow.
findings.fix_suggestion: Audit readNext relationships to ensure they're reciprocal where appropriate. Add validation check in tests to flag one-directional links.
findings.evidence: Blog article data structure uses readNext: ['slug-of-article'] array, but no validation or enforcement that B also links back to A.
findings.impact: One-directional linking wastes link juice. AI models can't identify strongly interrelated content clusters. Weaker topical authority signals.

findings.title: No breadcrumb schema on study and reflection pages
findings.description: Blog and book pages have BreadcrumbList schema, but /study, /plans, /reflections, and /insights pages don't. Breadcrumbs help search engines understand page hierarchy and AI models understand navigation context.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add breadcrumb schema to study, plans, and reflections layout files with proper position and item hierarchy.
findings.evidence: Study page renders content but doesn't include breadcrumb schema. Blog and books pages include proper breadcrumbJsonLd, but /study, /plans, /reflections lack this.
findings.impact: Reduces SEO signals about page hierarchy. AI models don't get structured nav context. Missing breadcrumb rich snippet in SERPs.

findings.title: Analytics script uses async, could use defer
findings.description: Plausible analytics script loads with async, which is correct, but could further optimize by deferring to not block any rendering. Minor optimization.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Change to defer:
<script defer src={siteConfig.plausibleScriptSrc}></script>
findings.evidence: <script async src={siteConfig.plausibleScriptSrc}></script>
findings.impact: Minimal - async is correct for analytics. Minor potential for faster LCP if script loads slightly later.

findings.title: Missing 'About' structured data (E-E-A-T signals)
findings.description: Site lacks AboutPage or Organization-level E-E-A-T signals. No clear markup for who created Selah, mission statement, or author credentials. AI models prefer clear E-E-A-T signals for religious/wellness content.
findings.fix_suggestion: Enhance Organization schema with E-E-A-T signals: name, url, description, sameAs, contactPoint, foundingDate. Consider adding /about or /mission page with team and values story.
findings.evidence: WebSite and WebApplication schema present, but no Organization schema with sameAs, founder, mission, or author info beyond footer tagline.
findings.impact: Weak E-E-A-T signals for AI models evaluating trustworthiness. No structured indication of who created this app. Matters for religious content where trust is paramount.

findings.title: No IndexNow integration for instant URL discovery
findings.description: Site uses sitemap.xml and robots.txt correctly, but doesn't integrate with Microsoft IndexNow. IndexNow allows instant discovery of new/updated content by search engines.
findings.fix_suggestion: Register for IndexNow at bing.com/indexnow. Add IndexNow key file or webhook handler. Call IndexNow API when publishing new blog articles.
findings.evidence: robots.ts includes sitemap reference but no IndexNow endpoint. No webhook endpoint configured to notify Bing/IndexNow of new blog articles.
findings.impact: Longer delay before new blog content gets indexed (typically 24-48 hours). IndexNow is optional but speeds up discovery of dynamic content.

findings.title: Card meanings page (/cards/) is client-rendered with no metadata export
findings.description: The /cards page (src/app/cards/page.tsx) is marked as 'use client' and does not export metadata. This is the primary SEO landing page for the site's largest content hub (78 card meaning pages). The page is listed in sitemap.xml with priority 0.9, yet search engines cannot see proper meta tags. The main content (card grid) is client-rendered only.
findings.fix_suggestion: Convert /cards/page.tsx to server-side page. Remove 'use client' directive and add metadata export with title, description, OG tags, and canonical URL. Move interactive filtering to a child component marked 'use client' if needed.
findings.evidence: 'use client'; // directive prevents metadata export
findings.impact: Critical ranking impact. Without proper meta tags, search result CTR is low. The page will not rank for 'tarot card meanings', 'learn tarot' and other high-intent queries. This prevents discovery of the entire card meanings section.

findings.title: Learn page content is hidden in JavaScript — education content completely inaccessible to crawlers
findings.description: The /learn page (src/app/learn/page.tsx) is a 'use client' component where all 20+ lessons (tarot basics, history, Major/Minor Arcana education, spreads, court cards, etc.) are stored in JavaScript state. The static HTML only contains placeholder sections. This is the largest on-site educational content asset and is invisible to search crawlers.
findings.fix_suggestion: Convert /learn to server-rendered multi-page structure using /learn/[section]/[lesson] routes with SSG, or pre-render all lessons as static HTML sections. This makes content HTML-crawlable while keeping accordion/expand UX.
findings.evidence: 'use client' with lessons defined in JS state, not rendered to HTML
findings.impact: Critical. This content targets 'how to learn tarot', 'tarot for beginners', 'major arcana explained' queries. Google cannot index it. AI search models cannot scrape the content. Massive missed opportunity for organic search.

findings.title: Missing datePublished and dateModified in Article schema — weakens E-E-A-T signals
findings.description: Blog articles and card pages include Article JSON-LD schema but lack datePublished and dateModified properties. These are critical E-E-A-T signals used by Google and AI models to assess content credibility and freshness.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add datePublished and dateModified properties to ArticleJsonLd schema in blog/[slug]/page.tsx and cards/[slug]/page.tsx. Store publication dates in article metadata and pass to schema function.
findings.evidence: ArticleJsonLd includes headline, description, url, publisher but missing datePublished and dateModified
findings.impact: High. Without dates, search engines cannot determine if content is recent. AI models prefer articles with publication dates. Featured snippets are more likely with complete Article schema.

findings.title: Card page OG images use static /og.svg instead of dynamic generation endpoint
findings.description: Card pages set OpenGraph image to static /og.svg. The /api/og Lambda endpoint exists and can generate dynamic, card-specific OG images with card symbol and meaning, but metadata points to generic static image.
findings.fix_suggestion: Update card metadata to use dynamic OG generation: /api/og?type=card&name=<cardname> endpoint. Ensure CloudFront routes /api/og to the Lambda function.
findings.evidence: images: [{ url: '/og.svg', width: 1200, height: 630 }] — same for all cards
findings.impact: High social sharing impact. Generic OG images reduce click-through rates on social media. Card-specific images would increase engagement.

findings.title: Missing internal linking in card meaning content — lost opportunity to build topical authority
findings.description: Card meaning pages show upright meaning, reversed meaning, and imagery in plain text with no internal links to related cards, spreads, or blog articles. The sidebar includes 'Learn to Read' links, but main content misses contextual cross-reference opportunities.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add contextual links in card meaning sections. Link to related cards (e.g., The Fool to The Magician), contrasting cards, and relevant blog guides. Use getRelatedCards() to auto-identify related pages.
findings.evidence: Upright Meaning and Reversed Meaning sections are plain paragraphs without internal links
findings.impact: Moderate to high. Internal linking distributes link equity and improves crawlability. AI models benefit from contextual cross-references. Poor linking reduces topical authority clustering.

findings.title: Missing FAQ schema for frequently asked questions — voice assistant optimization opportunity
findings.description: Blog articles (e.g., 'How to Read Tarot Cards', 'Reversed Tarot Cards Explained') contain multiple h2 headings structured as Q&A but don't implement FAQPage schema. This is valuable for voice assistants and AI model training.
findings.fix_suggestion: For FAQ-style blog content, add FAQPage schema wrapping the Q&A pairs. Extract h2 headers and paragraphs into structured FAQ format. Not all articles need this, only those with clear question/answer structure.
findings.evidence: Blog articles use h2 headings as questions ('What Tarot Actually Is', 'Choosing Your First Deck') without FAQPage schema
findings.impact: Medium. Voice assistants (Alexa, Google Assistant) prefer FAQPage schema. Featured snippets for 'how to' queries are more likely with proper schema.

findings.title: Privacy page canonical URL missing trailing slash — conflicts with site configuration
findings.description: Privacy page canonical is set to 'https://thenewbeautifulme.com/privacy' (no slash), but next.config.ts has trailingSlash: true, making actual URL '/privacy/'. This mismatch creates ambiguity for crawlers.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add trailing slash to canonical URL: 'https://thenewbeautifulme.com/privacy/'
findings.evidence: canonical: 'https://thenewbeautifulme.com/privacy' (missing trailing slash)
findings.impact: Medium. Canonical mismatch can split link equity between slash and non-slash versions.

findings.title: Missing author attribution and sameAs links — weak E-E-A-T signals
findings.description: Blog articles include Article schema but lack author field or sameAs properties linking to social profiles. This weakens E-E-A-T signals used by Google and AI models.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add author and sameAs properties to ArticleJsonLd with Organization name and links to Twitter, Instagram, and other social profiles.
findings.evidence: ArticleJsonLd defines publisher but no author field or sameAs linking to social profiles
findings.impact: Medium. E-E-A-T increasingly important for search ranking. Missing author signals reduce perceived organizational authority.

findings.title: Favicon defined as SVG only — missing PNG fallback for older browsers
findings.description: Favicon is moon-favicon.svg only. PNG fallback is a best practice for compatibility with older browsers.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add PNG and ICO favicon fallbacks for better compatibility.
findings.evidence: Only SVG favicon defined, no PNG or ICO fallback
findings.impact: Low. SVG support is excellent in modern browsers. Impact is minimal.

findings.title: Blog article OG images use static fallback — dynamic generation available but unused
findings.description: Blog articles use '/og.svg' for all OG images. The /api/og endpoint supports dynamic generation but is underutilized for blog content.
findings.fix_suggestion: Optional enhancement: Update blog OG images to use dynamic generation with article title. Not critical — current setup is acceptable.
findings.evidence: images: [{ url: '/og.svg', width: 1200, height: 630 }] for all blog articles
findings.impact: Low. Generic OG images reduce social CTR slightly, but impact is modest.
